result:
The plan is ready for your review. It creates 13 files under `extensions/x-twitter/` following the exact patterns from the Nostr and Twitch extensions:

- **OAuth 1.0a signing** with `node:crypto` (no external SDK dependency)
- **Probe** via `GET /2/users/me`
- **Send DMs** via `POST /2/dm_conversations/with/:id/messages`
- **Receive DMs** via polling `GET /2/dm_events` (15s default interval)
- **Onboarding** wizard prompting for 4 OAuth credentials
- **Unit tests** matching the Nostr test pattern exactly

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: X/Twitter DM Channel Extension

## Context

Add a new channel extension at `extensions/x-twitter/` that enables sending and receiving X/Twitter DMs via the X API v2. This follows the established channel extension patterns used by Nostr, Twitch, IRC, and other extensions in the repo.

## Files to Create

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `extensions/x-twitter/package.json` | Package metadata + `openclaw.channel` config |
| `extensions/x-twitter/openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest (`id`, `channels`, `configSchema`) |
| `extensions/x-twitter/index.ts` | Entry point: `register()` sets runtime, registers channel |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/runtime.ts` | `get/setXTwitterRuntime` singleton (same as Nostr pattern) |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/types.ts` | `XTwitterAccountConfig`, `ResolvedXTwitterAccount`, account resolution helpers |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/config-schema.ts` | Zod schema for `channels.x-twitter.*` config |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/x-api.ts` | OAuth 1.0a signing + `xApiFetch` helper (uses `node:crypto`, no SDK) |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/probe.ts` | `probeXTwitter()`: `GET /2/users/me` health check |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/outbound.ts` | `sendXTwitterDm()`: `POST /2/dm_conversations/with/:id/messages` |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/poll-bus.ts` | DM polling loop with dedup (gateway inbound) |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/onboarding.ts` | `ChannelOnboardingAdapter` for credential prompts |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/channel.ts` | Main `ChannelPlugin<ResolvedXTwitterAccount>` definition |
| `extensions/x-twitter/src/channel.test.ts` | Unit tests for plugin shape, config, messaging, outbound |

## Reference Files

- `extensions/nostr/src/channel.ts` — Primary pattern for ChannelPlugin shape, adapters
- `extensions/nostr/src/types.ts` — Account resolution pattern (listAccountIds, resolveAccount)
- `extensions/nostr/src/runtime.ts` — Runtime singleton pattern
- `extensions/nostr/src/config-schema.ts` — Zod config schema pattern
- `extensions/nostr/src/channel.test.ts` — Test pattern
- `extensions/twitch/src/onboarding.ts` — Onboarding adapter pattern with credential prompts
- `extensions/irc/src/probe.ts` — Simple probe implementation pattern

## Implementation Details

### Channel ID & Config Path
- Channel ID: `"x-twitter"`
- Config path: `channels.x-twitter`
- Package name: `@openclaw/x-twitter`

### Capabilities
```
chatTypes: ["direct"]   // DMs only for MVP
media: false            // Text DMs only
```

### Credentials (OAuth 1.0a User Context)
Stored in `channels.x-twitter`:
- `apiKey` — Consumer Key
- `apiKeySecret` — Consumer Secret
- `accessToken` — User Access Token
- `accessTokenSecret` — User Access Token Secret

### Adapters to Implement
- **config** — `listAccountIds`, `resolveAccount`, `isConfigured`, `describeAccount`, `resolveAllowFrom`, `formatAllowFrom`
- **security** — `resolveDmPolicy` (pairing/allowlist/open/disabled)
- **pairing** — `idLabel: "xUserId"`, `normalizeAllowEntry`
- **messaging** — `normalizeTarget`, `targetResolver` (numeric X user IDs)
- **outbound** — `deliveryMode: "direct"`, `sendText` via X API v2 DM endpoint, `textChunkLimit: 10000`
- **status** — `defaultRuntime`, `collectStatusIssues`, `buildAccountSnapshot`, `probeAccount` (delegates to `probeXTwitter`)
- **gateway** — `startAccount` starts poll loop, returns stop handle
- **onboarding** — `getStatus`, `configure` with credential prompts + env var detection

### X API v2 Endpoints
- **Probe**: `GET https://api.x.com/2/users/me` → `{ data: { id, name, username } }`
- **Send DM**: `POST https://api.x.com/2/dm_conversations/with/:participant_id/messages` → `{ data: { dm_conversation_id, dm_event_id } }`
- **Poll DMs**: `GET https://api.x.com/2/dm_events?event_types=MessageCreate` → paginated events

### OAuth 1.0a Signing (`x-api.ts`)
Uses `node:crypto` HMAC-SHA1 — no external SDK. ~80 lines for signature generation:
1. Collect oauth params (consumer_key, nonce, signature_method, timestamp, token, version)
2. Build base string: `METHOD&percent_encoded_url&percent_encoded_sorted_params`
3. Sign with `HMAC-SHA1(consumer_secret&token_secret, base_string)`
4. Build `Authorization: OAuth ...` header

### Polling Bus (`poll-bus.ts`)
- Default 15-second interval (configurable via `pollIntervalSec`)
- Tracks seen event IDs in a bounded Set (~5000 entries, FIFO eviction)
- Filters out own messages by comparing `sender_id` to self user ID
- Calls `onMessage(senderId, text, eventId)` for new inbound DMs

### Tests (`channel.test.ts`)
Following `extensions/nostr/src/channel.test.ts` exactly:
- Plugin meta (id, label, docsPath, blurb)
- Capabilities (direct only, no group, no media)
- Config adapter (functions exist, listAccountIds empty/configured, resolveAccount configured flag)
- Messaging (target resolver recognizes numeric IDs, rejects non-numeric)
- Outbound (delivery mode, text chunk limit)
- Security (resolveDmPolicy exists)
- Gateway (startAccount exists)
- Status (defaultRuntime, buildAccountSnapshot)

### Peripheral Changes
Add to `.github/labeler.yml`:
```yaml
"channel: x-twitter":
  - changed-files:
      - any-glob-to-any-file:
          - "extensions/x-twitter/**"
```

## Implementation Order

1. Create scaffold: `package.json`, `openclaw.plugin.json`, `index.ts`, `src/runtime.ts`
2. Define types: `src/types.ts`, `src/config-schema.ts`
3. Build X API helpers: `src/x-api.ts` (OAuth 1.0a signing)
4. Implement probe: `src/probe.ts`
5. Implement outbound: `src/outbound.ts`
6. Build channel plugin: `src/channel.ts` (assembles all adapters)
7. Implement polling bus: `src/poll-bus.ts`
8. Implement onboarding: `src/onboarding.ts`
9. Write tests: `src/channel.test.ts`
10. Update `.github/labeler.yml`

## Verification

1. `pnpm install` — ensure new extension is recognized
2. `pnpm test extensions/x-twitter` — run unit tests
3. `pnpm tsgo` — type-check passes
4. `pnpm check` — lint/format passes
